key_insight: Discrete, tensor-native STDP inference choices can materially improve visual place recognition, with neuron assignment, reset, and frame aggregation jointly driving recall at perfect precision.
engineering_relevance: For autonomous navigation, the paper highlights that low-level inference design decisions in spiking neural networks can be as important as the learning rule itself for reliable loop-closure performance.
method: Implemented a discrete PyTorch/snnTorch STDP-based SNN-VPR pipeline.
Compared deterministic closed-form neuron assignment against standard argmax assignment.
Ablated query-wise state reset and velocity-compensated sliding-window aggregation over k frames.
evidence_signal: On the 100-place Nordland dataset with 15 independently trained networks, the approach reached R@100P = 100.00% at k = 5 for constant-velocity traversal, with only 0.20 ms added latency.
watch_item: Some of the reported gain is confounded by implementation differences from prior continuous-time models, so the exact contribution of each mechanism is not fully isolated.
paper_title: Visual Place Recognition Using Rate-Encoded Spiking Neural Networks with Discrete STDP Learning
